Charles De Ketelaere leaps in the air in celebration of a goal

articles.title: Trump admits he asked FIFA to review US striker's red card

children.content: Football's European governing body blasts the decision to suspend a US striker's World Cup red-card ban, saying FIFA had "crossed a red line" after US President Donald Trump personally intervened in the case.

image.altText: A man holds a camera up to take a selfie with a second man smiling beside.

articles.title: Severe blowback over FIFA's 'shameful, incomprehensible' Balogun decision

children.content: FIFA has always insisted it is politically neutral. But its integrity is now being questioned after US President Donald Trump confirmed he asked for Folarin Balogun's suspension to be reviewed.

image.altText: Folarin in his socer strip on the field

children.content: Folarin Balogun will start for the US against Belgium, while Spain's Mikel Merino scores in injury time to snatch a 1-0 win over Portugal in a cagey affair in Dallas. Follow live.

items.title: Belgium ignores Balogun controversy to dominate opening half against USA

updates.title: HT: Belgium well on top of nervy USA in Seattle

items.title: Microsoft workers in Australia to be caught up in 4,800 global job cuts

children.content: ABC News understands Microsoft Australia staff will be impacted by the tech giant's latest round of lay-offs. Microsoft will cut about 2.1 per cent of its global workforce.

image.altText: Microsoft logo

items.title: Donald Trump teaches crypto devotees a valuable lesson

children.content: US President Donald Trump should have been a godsend to the mysterious world of cryptocurrency. Instead, it has been a godsend to him.

image.altText: Illustration of Donald Trump holding a bitcoin

images.altText: A rocket launches from the sea leaving smoke in its wake.

articles.title: Chinese nuclear-capable missile appears to land close to Nauru, Tuvalu

children.content: The head of Taiwan's national security council says China is "again" proving to be the "bully of the block".

image.altText: A suspected trajectory of a Chinese ballistic missile.

articles.title: China warns Australia over landmark military alliance in the Pacific

children.content: China warns Australia not to harm its interests in the Pacific after Anthony Albanese strikes a landmark military alliance with Fiji.

image.altText: A woman with short black hair stands in front of a podium with the Chinese flag behind her.

children.content: Defence Industry Minister Pat Conroy says Australia was monitoring a Chinese navy task force group that was involved in tracking a long-range missile test. Follow live.

items.title: Australia was monitoring Chinese navy taskforce group ahead of missile test, minister says

updates.title: Solomon Islands PM references China's ballistic missile test
Angus Taylor labels China's missile test 'unwelcome provocation'
Jacinta Nampijinpa Price labels PM's comments on Kylie Minogue 'misogynistic'

items.title: WiseTech's Richard White to step down as chair, but remain on board

children.content: Shares in WiseTech global are surging in early trade. Wall Street has closed at a record level as oil prices settle to where they were before the war in Iran. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Richard White seated at a wooden boardroom table with a tablet in front of him.

items.title: Boys major victims of AI-assisted online sexual abuse, study finds

children.content: About one in every 25 Australians under the age of 18 has been the victim or knows a victim of artificial intelligence-assisted online sexual abuse, according to a new study.

image.altText: A maths student studies with a calculator on their desk.

items.title: After months of Trump threats, Cuba says he's committing 'genocide'

children.content: The lives of ordinary Cubans have ground to a halt under an economic and humanitarian crisis that has developed over years and been aggravated by US President Donald Trump's harsh restrictions, experts say.

gallery.altText: A blue tricycle with a trailer driving past colourful buildings and a 'Cuba' sign.

thumbnail.altText: A blue tricycle with a trailer driving past colourful buildings and a 'Cuba' sign.

items.title: Families told accused Bali bomber Hambali to face 202 life terms

children.content: US military prosecutors reveal new details about their case against the man accused of planning the 2002 Bali bombings, telling the loved ones of Australian victims his trial could finally begin next year.

image.altText: A bespectacled middle aged Indonesian man with a grey beard but no moustache wears a black and white top.

items.title: Ex-champion boxer charged over four kidnappings

children.content: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged 26-year-old Humzah Khalid, previously known by his boxing name The Raging Bull, for his alleged involvement in four kidnappings over 10 months.

image.altText: A man wearing boxing gloves and a black shirt looks serious as he stands in a boxing ring with posters on the walls.

items.title: 'A lot of stigma': The 'taboo' cancer side effects we don't talk about

children.content: Shame and stigma mean gynaecological side effects from cancer treatment — affecting women's sexual, urinary and reproductive health — are under-reported and under-researched.

image.altText: A woman speaks on a podium, she has short curly hair and a serious expression.

items.title: 'Incredibly vulnerable' industry becomes new target for scammers

children.content: Amidst a growing number of people taking up gig work to cope with high living costs, scammers are stealing the earnings of "already struggling" delivery drivers.

image.altText: A caucasian man wearing a dark green tshirt stands in front of a white car posing with a serious look

items.title: Jai Arrow marries partner months after MND diagnosis

children.content: The former Rabbitohs and Queensland NRL player has married his partner Berina Colakovic in a small ceremony in Sydney, months after revealing his motor neurone disease diagnosis.

gallery.altText: A man and woman kiss at their wedding.

thumbnail.altText: A man and woman kiss at their wedding.

items.title: Pam the Bird graffiti vandal detained after bridge standoff

children.content: A trespasser has been detained by police after spending many hours at the top of a 140- metre-high tower of the Bolte Bridge in Melbourne where he'd spray-painted the surface with the notorious graffiti symbol.

image.altText: A man clad in black abseils along a pylon on Melbourne's Bolte Bridge.

items.title: Alex de Minaur suffers another 'truly gut-wrenching' Wimbledon loss

children.content: Alex de Minaur loses in the fourth round at Wimbledon in another match he feels he could, and maybe should, have won against ninth seed Flavio Cobolli.

image.altText: Alex de Minaur looks miserable at Wimbledon.

items.title: Use your phone for work? This is how you can claim it as a tax deduction

children.content: The cost of buying and using your own phone may be tax deductible, but the ATO has specific rules about how employees calculate these expenses.

gallery.altText: Close up view of young women using a smartphone on a couch in living room.

thumbnail.altText: Close up view of young women using a smartphone on a couch in living room.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Your challenge in today's ABC Places quiz is to work out the location of 10 photos from around the world. Be warned: we might try to trick you.

image.altText: A cow, a pig and a horse are cut into a corn field, seen from above.

items.title: Celebrate NAIDOC Week with these free films and TV series

children.content: From Bluey in Yolŋu Matha to the first feature film directed by an Indigenous woman, there is a trove of free movies and TV to dive into this NAIDOC Week.

image.altText: A TV still of Megan Wilding and Tony Armstrong, both mid-30s, Indigenous, in a TV studio, Wilding dressed as a silver snake.

items.title: 'Disappointing': Prince Harry and king's awkward stand-off over palace

children.content: The strained relationship between Prince Harry and his father, King Charles III, has been laid bare once again, after an awkward stand-off over accommodation at Buckingham Palace.

gallery.altText: A redheaded man with a beard

thumbnail.altText: A redheaded man with a beard

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Company pledges $500m for net zero mill to resurrect 'steel city'

children.content: Once dubbed Australia's "steel city", steel production wound down in Newcastle in 1999 when BHP closed its plant. Now, an Australian company says it will build a new steel plant, powered only by electricity.

image.altText: a digital mock up of a modern steel facility

items.title: Bridget Archer standing in for Rockliff is a soft-launched succession plan

children.content: Make no mistake, news that maverick Liberal Bridget Archer will be acting Tasmanian premier for a week is a significant step towards her eventually taking over from Jeremy Rockliff — a move sure to enrage those who believe they have a more rightful claim.

image.altText: Bridget Archer and Jeremy Rockliff sit next to each other on a park bench.

items.title: Democrats' Platner accused of sexual assault, dashing party's US Senate hopes

children.content: Maine Senate candidate Graham Platner says the allegation is "categorically false", but is considering his next steps as the report prompts several key backers to withdraw their endorsements.

image.altText: A middle-aged white man with a beard and a sleeve tattoo speaks passionately at a campaign event.

items.title: Mushroom murder trial judge shares story in podcast

children.content: Retired judge Christopher Beale has reflected on his long legal career, his temporary pivot to becoming a Jesuit priest and the role he played in the Erin Patterson murder trial.

image.altText: A judge wearing traditional court garb speaks into a microphone.

items.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

articles.title: From Call of Duty to Dreamtime: These designers are lighting up video games

children.content: As part of NAIDOC Week, we meet the Indigenous creatives who are re-shaping Australia's video game industry.

image.altText: Two men stand with black t-shirts, one man on the left is playing a VR headset.

articles.title: 'Oldest continually practised medicine' being passed on to students

children.content: Aboriginal Australians have used native plants as bush medicine for tens of thousands of years. On Larrakia land, medical students are learning how nature can heal.

image.altText: A man wearing a brown hat takes a bite of a small passionfruit.

items.title: Australia's Indian community prepares to put on a show for PM's visit

children.content: On a grey July day, a Melbourne community hall is bursting with colour as Australia's Indian community gears up to welcome Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

image.altText: Smiling women in red blouses, cream sarees, large bindis, hair tied, have hand behind hair as they sing.

items.title: Trump and the European leaders he berated to come face-to-face in Türkiye

children.content: Dozens of world leaders are descending on Türkiye this week to rubber-stamp significant NATO commitments, but the dynamics on the sidelines could prove more interesting to watch.

image.altText: A man in a suit looking on and gesturing with his hand. He is seen from close up.

items.title: 'Longstanding injustice': Doubts cast over guilt for three of 'Croatian Six'

children.content: An inquiry has raised "reasonable doubt" over the guilt of three of six Croatian men jailed in 1981 over an alleged terrorist plot to bomb several Sydney buildings in 1979.

children.content: They waited four years for a court outcome, only to be left feeling like it was an injustice.

items.title: Will government vouchers turn around slow start to Kimberley's tourist season?

children.content: The WA government says a discounted tours scheme will help revitalise the Kimberley's sluggish start to its annual tourism season but operators have questioned the voucher program.

children.content: Victoria's workplace safety watchdog is investigating after a suburban footy player was injured, and later died, after his head collided with a cricket pitch.

items.title: Germany cracks down on 'extraordinary' rate of sick leave

children.content: Chucking a sickie is no longer as easy as a quick phone call in Germany, under new laws hoped to boost productivity. Here's how the world is responding.
